grammar.name: ✅ Finite Indicative Active Past Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the past tense, e.g., 'I was...', 'I chose...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I was nourished by the Earth Mother's fertile hands
I chose to embrace the wild woman's abundance
I found my strength in the cyclical rhythms of creation

grammar.name: ⭐ Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am nourished by the abundance of my own heart
I trust that my natural rhythms will guide my growth
I am worthy of the deep connection that the Wild Woman offers

grammar.name: ⏭️ Finite Indicative Active Future Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the future tense with 'will': 'I will...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I will nurture my own abundance with the same care I offer to others
I will embrace the cyclical rhythm of creation and destruction as a natural truth
I will find peace by loving myself and my community with the same tenderness

examples: I have been building my resilience.
I have been growing every day.
grammar.name: 🔄 Finite Indicative Active Present Perfect Progressive First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the perfect progressive: 'I have been [verb]ing...'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I have been nurturing my own spirit with love
I have been choosing to embrace the wild woman's rhythm
I have been growing every day in abundance

examples: I will have overcome this challenge.
I will have become the person I am meant to be.
I will have created a life I love.
grammar.name: 🎯 Finite Indicative Active Future Perfect Perfective First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the future perfect tense: 'I will have [past participle]...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I will have become the woman who nurtures the earth with her soft hands
I will have created a life of abundance that flows from my own heart
I will have returned to the natural rhythms of creation and destruction that I was meant to live

grammar.name: 🤝 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Plural
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'we' (first person plural).

affirmations.text: We nurture our own abundance with every breath
We embrace our own vitality in every step
We create our own richness in every moment

examples: They believe in their own power.
grammar.name: 👤 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple Third
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is third-person: 'he', 'she', 'it', or 'they'.

affirmations.text: I am a fertile seed that nurtures the earth with its own tenderness
I am the wild woman who breathes life into every cycle of creation
I am the Earth Mother who sustains abundance through the gentle care of my own heart

examples: I can handle whatever comes my way.
I could become who I dream of being.
grammar.name: 💪 Finite Potential Active Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Express capability or possibility using 'can' or 'could'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I can nurture my own growth with the same care I offer to others
I am ready to embrace the wild woman who brings abundance to my life
I can become the fertile earth mother who sustains my daily rhythms

examples: May I find clarity in this moment.
If only I could feel at peace.
Would that I might know my own worth.
grammar.name: 🙏 Finite Optative Active First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Express a wish or hope, e.g., 'May I find...', 'If only I could...'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: May I find abundance in this moment
If only I could nurture my own heart
Would that I might feel connected to the Earth Mother

grammar.name: ❗ Finite Imperative Active Second Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- Give a direct command (imperative mood), e.g., 'Be bold!'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Subject/address is 'you' (second person).

affirmations.text: Be bold! You are the Earth Mother who nurtures all life with your fertile hands
Trust yourself! Your wild woman spirit is the source of your true abundance
Embrace your greatness! You are the fecund fruit that brings life to the cycle of creation

examples: Am I not capable of this?
Have I not already proven my resilience?
Can I not rise to this challenge?
grammar.name: ❓ Finite Interrogative Active Present First Singular Negative
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- CRITICAL: The sentence MUST be phrased as a QUESTION ending with '?', e.g., 'Am I not...?', 'Have I not...?', 'Can I not...?'. Do NOT write a declarative statement.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: Am I not nourished by the Earth Mother's fertile hands?
Have I not already found abundance in the wild woman's embrace?
Can I not rise to this challenge without the love of my own creation?

grammar.name: ♾️ Infinitive Active Simple
grammar.description:
- Start with the base infinitive: 'To [verb]...' — no subject or conjugation.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.

affirmations.text: To nourish the earth with the fruit of abundance
To embrace the wild woman with the care of the Earth Mother
To live fully by the rhythm of creation and destruction

grammar.name: 💡 Gerund Active
grammar.description:
- Begin with a gerund (-ing form as a noun), e.g., 'Being brave is...'.
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.

affirmations.text: Being nourished by the Earth Mother is my strength
Choosing abundance in every moment is my strength
Embracing the wild woman's rhythm is my strength

examples: I am supported by those around me.
grammar.name: 🌊 Finite Indicative Passive Present Simple First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- CRITICAL: Use PASSIVE voice — the subject receives the action. Pattern: 'I am/was/have been [past participle]', e.g., 'I am loved', 'I am guided', 'I am supported by...'. Do NOT write active sentences like 'I trust' or 'I choose'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am nourished by the Earth Mother's fertile earth
I am guided by the wild woman's deep connection
I am supported by the abundance of the fat and fruit

examples: I consistently show up for myself.
grammar.name: 🔁 Finite Indicative Active Present Habitual First Singular
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Express a recurring action using 'always', 'regularly', 'consistently', or 'every day'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I always nurture my own spirit with the same care I give to others
I consistently embrace abundance through the rhythms of creation and destruction
I always choose to be the Wild Woman who radiates fecundity and richness

examples: I am not defined by my past.
I am not afraid of change.
I do not limit my own potential.
grammar.name: 🫷 Finite Indicative Active Present Simple First Singular Negative
grammar.description:
- Use a fully conjugated (finite) verb that agrees with its subject.
- State a direct fact or present truth (indicative mood).
- Use ACTIVE voice: the subject ('I'/'we') performs the action.
- Include negation using 'not', 'never', 'no longer', or 'without'.
- Use the present tense, e.g., 'I am...', 'I trust...'.
- Subject is 'I' (first person singular).

affirmations.text: I am not defined by my past
I am not afraid of change
I do not limit my own potential
